brief_justification: Agent got 883 vs correct 958 by incorrectly allowing Sub B to export 50MW spare capacity via tie line to City A at t=30+, a constraint error that compounds over the 90-min final interval; agent correctly decomposed into time intervals and traced the cascade but missed the tie-line directionality constraint that drives the correct answer; agent restated its own reasoning framework without monitoring its unverified assumption that the tie line is bidirectional at t=30; agent tabulated all intervals but never tested the tie-line export premise from an independent angle or boundary condition; agent presented Sub B's 50MW export to City A as a logical fact without flagging it as an assumption dependent on tie-line directionality; agent did not consider the alternative where Sub B serves only its own city or where tie-line flow is unidirectional; all interval calculations are shown in a reproducible table with derivations, making the chain auditable even if the premise is wrong; the spatial-scale negative gate (map resolution blur from micro details) is not triggered since the agent stayed at a consistent engineering abstraction level throughout; the LOD and detail-aliasing suppress terms from the injection do not map onto power grid analysis, and the agent showed no evidence of those failure modes; agent completely replaced the injected spatial-scale classification DAG topology with its own linear interval-mapping approach, showing zero alignment with the branching/gating/looping structure of the provided topology.
